steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a subject ('The atomic structure of an element') and an object ('the element's place on the periodic table'). The blank must be a finite main verb that connects them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to determine': an infinitive cannot serve as the main verb of a sentence by itself. ✗
  • Choice B — 'determining': a present participle cannot serve as the main verb without a helping verb. ✗
  • Choice C — 'determines': a finite third-person singular verb that agrees with the singular subject and completes the sentence grammatically. ✓
  • Choice D — 'having determined': a perfect participle cannot serve as the main verb by itself. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
